title: Scout briefing format lacks optional Errors section placement
Scout ready mode gaps: Step 4 references bd blocked not run, assumption undocumented
dependency-analysis.md says parallel bd show but scout.md uses sequential write-each-immediately
description: Root cause: orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:58 says '(parallel bd show calls)' but scout.md Step 3 uses sequential write-each-immediately pattern for partial progress safety. Inconsistent guidance. Fix: Qualify or remove 'parallel' mention in dependency-analysis.md to match scout.md's sequential approach. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: Scout Step 2.5 silently skips agent files with invalid frontmatter
scout.md Step 3 does not explicitly say to continue iteration after bd show failure
description: Root cause: orchestration/templates/scout.md:77-80 Step 3 writes metadata per task. Error handling (lines 200-204) says to write error metadata on bd show failure, but Step 3 prose does not say to continue to the next task. A fresh Scout might stop on first failure. Fix: Add 'If bd show fails, write error metadata and continue to next task' in Step 3. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: Scout metadata write-each-immediately has no truncation/atomicity check downstream
Scout Step 5.5 coverage verification missing wave capacity validation
scout.md PICK ONE bracket syntax resembles regex/BNF notation
description:
Root cause: scout.md:118-119 uses 'PICK ONE: [type-a | type-b]' format for tie-breaking results. The bracket-pipe syntax resembles regex or BNF notation and could be confused with optional/alternative notation rather than a literal list.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/templates/scout.md:118-119

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F6). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Use plainer format like 'PICK ONE: debugger OR performance-engineer'.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Tie-breaking format uses plain English connectors instead of bracket notation
